name: the-wealth-of-nations
description: Adam Smith's foundational work of classical economics — the division of labor, prices and value, wages-profit-rent, capital accumulation, and the progress of opulence from feudalism to commerce.
useCase: Understanding the division of labor, price theory, income distribution, capital and investment, the historical progress of opulence, and the foundations of free-market economics.
knownRisks: Books I-III only — Books IV-V (mercantilism, physiocracy, public finance) are not included in this edition
Smith's 18th-century context means some assumptions (e.g., the wage fund) are no longer accepted by modern economics
The Analytical Introduction is by Andrew Skinner, not Smith — it reflects a modern scholarly interpretation
The labor theory of value was later critiqued and refined by David Ricardo and Karl Marx
